Ad Text:
Lead .NET Developer - AI Solutions | Shape the Future of AI at Buildxact

Melbourne, AU (Hybrid) | Engineering Team | Greenfield Projects + Real Impact


About Buildxact
Buildxact is a leader in the construction technology industry and is at the forefront of delivering impactful construction management software used by builders worldwide. We help small to medium builders run better businesses through smarter estimating, job management, and connected workflows.

At Buildxact, AI is product-critical.

As we continue to invest heavily in AI-powered product innovation, we’re looking for a Lead .NET Developer – AI Solutions to play a key role in shaping and delivering the next generation of intelligent capabilities across our platform.


The Opportunity
This is a true hands-on senior engineering role where you’ll lead the design and delivery of AI-driven product solutions, working closely with Product Managers, Designers, and fellow Engineers.

You’ll take ideas from concept through to production and apply modern AI technologies—including LLMs, machine learning models, and retrieval‑augmented generation (RAG)—to solve real customer problems.

While this role does not have direct reports, it carries strong technical leadership expectations: mentoring others, uplifting team capability, and helping define best practices for AI within Buildxact.

Why you’ll love it here

AI Greenfield Momentum – Shape greenfield AI solutions and new product lines with real ownership. Work on meaningful, production AI—not experiments

Real Impact – Everything you ship is used by builders in the real world

Modern Stack – C#, ASP.NET Core, EF Core and the .NET ecosystem (including MCP, Semantic Kernel and Microsoft Agent Framework)

Strong Team Culture – High‑performing, collaborative, ego‑free, and big on learning


What you’ll be doing

Design, build, and deliver new product solutions with a strong focus on AI and machine learning

Lead the implementation of AI capabilities, including LLMs, prompt/context engineering, RAG, and model evaluation

Partner with Product Managers to explore what’s possible with AI and translate ideas into scalable solutions

Define and maintain test harnesses to assess AI output quality and compare models

Contribute to the evolution of Buildxact’s .NET-based product architecture

Ensure high-quality delivery through strong testing practices (automated and manual)

Share knowledge, mentor engineers, and uplift AI capability across the development team

Stay current with the rapidly evolving AI and LLM ecosystem and bring learnings back to the team


You’ll thrive if you have:

7+ years of experience as a software engineer using .NET framework in delivering and building full-stack products in a SaaS, cloud-first environment, with a strong track record of ownership and technical leadership

Proven experience in integrating LLMs and machine learning into production systems

Proven capability in prompt and context engineering, with an iterative, quality‑driven approach

Deep expertise in C#, ASP.NET Core, EF Core, and the .NET ecosystem (Semantic Kernel, MCP, or Microsoft Agent Framework highly regarded)

Experience with Azure or strong motivation to learn and adopt it quickly

Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Claude Code, Cursor) and their practical use

Experience in technologies such as Angular, TypeScript, Flutter, GraphQL/HotChocolate, or vision models (e.g. Roboflow) would be desirable.

A collaborative mindset, customer focus, and passion for building high‑quality software


The Buildxact Way:
We build like the people we serve: with simplicity, grit, and purpose.

Our values are:
It’s yours to nail
Customer success is our success
Do what’s right
Keep it simple

Ready to help shape the future of AI at Buildxact?
Apply now with your CV and a short cover letter on what excites you about building AI‑powered products.

Salary: No contractor fees / two pay runs per week

Citect SCADA
Experience with industrial communication protocols (Profibus, Profinet, Ethernet-based I/O).

Crane automation systems.
GEM80 PLCs.

If you are seeking a career that allows you to make a real impact in the world of Engineering on a long-term contract with excellent $$$, then we want to speak to you.

Interested? APPLY NOW!!!

At the heart of Re.Group is our focus on positive community impact. We collaborate with charities, private organisations, and Government to benefit local communities.

We’re looking for a TM1 & Microsoft to strengthen our analytics, planning and reporting capability across the business. If you enjoy solving complex data challenges and building high-quality financial and operational models, this role offers a unique opportunity to make an impact.

Design, develop and maintain TM1/Planning Analytics cubes, dimensions, rules, feeders and TI processes

Manage TM1 system administration, performance, user roles and security

At Re.Group, we celebrate diversity, equity, and inclusion. Our commitment is to foster working environments that embrace everyone, ensuring fair and respectful treatment for all.

Job Title: Senior Backend Engineer - Video AI Engine
Ad Text:
Job Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

What you’d be doing in this role

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

At the moment, this role is focused on:

Building and scaling backend services supporting video AI infrastructure

Developing robust APIs and pipelines for video model inference

Optimising systems for latency, reliability, and throughput

Collaborating with MLEs, Researchers, and Product teams to bring new capabilities to life

Ensuring observability and smooth operations of video services

Bringing a pragmatic and product-focused mindset to backend development
 

You're probably a match if

You have experience building and scaling distributed backend systems

You have strong programming skills in a backend language (e.g., Java, Go, Python)

You are familiar with cloud infrastructure (e.g., AWS, GCP) and service orchestration

You have exposure to machine learning workflows or serving systems

You have strong collaboration skills and a growth mindset

You have a passion for building elegant systems that unlock creative potential at scale
 

About the team

The Video AI Engine team sits within the Video group in the Design Experience supergroup. We build the infrastructure that connects advanced AI research with delightful product experiences in Canva’s video editor. From video segmentation and effect orchestration to scalable ML serving, we’re enabling users to create cinematic stories, automatically and effortlessly.

As a Senior Backend Engineer, you’ll design and build the distributed systems that serve as the backbone of Canva’s AI-powered video features. You’ll work on performance-critical pipelines, collaborate with ML Engineers to productionize models, and contribute to the reliability and scalability of Canva’s video platform.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora,你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point. 

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney. This role is preferred to be based out of our Sydney office, where the majority of this team is located. 

Job Description


About the Group/Team

Canva Research – Design Generation

We’re building the systems that turn imagination into reality. Within Canva Research, the Design Generation group focuses on creating AI models and pipelines that produce beautiful designs for Canvas users. We work at the intersection of research and product, partnering closely with Product, Design, and Engineering to ship AI-powered experiences that feel magical, reliable, and deeply human. Our work directly shapes how users create, explore, and express ideas in Canva every day.

About the Role/Specialty:

As a Senior Research Engineer, we sit at the boundary between research and production. We explore new ideas in generative modelling, evaluate their real-world impact, and turn promising research into scalable systems that power Canva’s design experiences. As a Senior Research Engineer, this role operates with a high degree of autonomy, solving complex problems, influencing technical direction, and collaborating across teams to deliver meaningful outcomes. Our success is measured not just by research quality, but by the impact our work has on real users.

What you’ll do (responsibilities):

You’ll design, experiment with, and evaluate generative AI models for design-focused use cases.

You’ll translate research outcomes into scalable, production-ready systems.

You’ll collaborate closely with Product, Design, and Engineering partners to align research with user needs.

You’ll analyse model performance using both quantitative metrics and qualitative user signals.

You’ll contribute to technical discussions, architecture decisions, and long-term AI strategy.

You’ll share learnings through documentation, reviews, and internal presentations to uplift the broader team.

What we're looking for:


You’re an accomplished applied researcher who loves seeing ideas land in the hands of real users. You’re comfortable navigating ambiguity, balancing research depth with practical constraints, and making thoughtful trade-offs. You bring solid experience in machine learning or generative modelling, enjoy collaborating across disciplines, and care deeply about quality, ethics, and impact. You’re motivated by solving meaningful problems at scale and excited by the challenge of building AI that empowers creativity.

Bachelor or Masters degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering or a related field, or equivalent skills gained through 3-5 years of work experience. Advanced qualifications including a PhD are an advantage.

Experience with generative models: Proficient in working with large language models (LLMs), diffusion models, and multimodal architectures to develop innovative solutions.

Skills in training generative models: Experienced in techniques such as fine-tuning, in-context learning, and reinforcement learning to optimise model performance.

Proficiency in deep learning frameworks: Skilled in leveraging tools like PyTorch, JAX, and HuggingFace for advanced research and experimentation.

Expertise in experimental design and evaluation: Capable of implementing experimental design strategies for generative models, using both quantitative metrics and qualitative analysis to assess outcomes.

Ability to enhance research tooling and workflows: Experienced in rapid prototyping, conducting ablation studies, and tracking experiments to streamline research processes.

Competence in data curation and analysis: Adept at managing and analysing datasets for training and evaluating generative systems.

Familiarity with current research literature: Skilled at reproducing impactful papers and applying novel techniques to address emerging problem spaces.

Additional Information


Don't tick all the boxes? Don't worry about that - nobody does!  

We’d still love to hear from you! At Canva, we know that great engineers come from a variety of backgrounds, and we value passion, curiosity, and a willingness to learn just as much as specific experience. If you're excited about this role but don’t tick every box, we encourage you to apply, you might a great fit in ways you didn’t expect!

What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a stack of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too
Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers
An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more
Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out  lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

Job Description


About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

What you’ll do

Lead and grow a high-performing team of Machine Learning Engineers focused on automating content review and moderation across Canva’s Content Library.

Set and execute the technical vision and strategy for ML-driven safety, quality, and policy enforcement across text, image, video, audio and template content.

Design and scale robust ML systems — including experimentation frameworks, evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and feedback loops to continuously improve model performance and close the gap between manual review and automation.

Partner cross-functionally with Content, Product, Review Ops, and Review Engineering to deliver impactful, user-facing solutions and scalable human-in-the-loop systems.

Drive large, end-to-end initiatives from problem framing and roadmap definition through to delivery, experimentation, rollout, and impact measurement.

Uplift engineering excellence through strong technical direction, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a healthy code review and knowledge-sharing culture.

Coach and mentor engineers to navigate complex technical challenges and do the best work of their lives.

Champion operational excellence, responsible AI, and safety-by-design, ensuring our systems serve all users, including students and educators, with quality, integrity, and trust.

Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging ML best practices, continuously evolving the domain to meet Canva’s scale and ambition.

You're probably a match if

You’re passionate about coaching and developing engineers, creating a culture of growth and support.

You’re experienced in designing systems at scale, with a focus on reliability, performance, and extensibility.

You collaborate cross-functionally and thrive in partnerships with product, ML, and content stakeholders.

You think strategically and deliver pragmatically, balancing long-term vision with immediate needs.

You value diversity, empathy, and inclusion, and lead in a way that fosters belonging and trust.

You have experience leading an engineering team in a Product or SaaS environment, ideally within ML or applied AI domains.

Prior experience in text, image, video, and audio understanding is highly desirable.

Additional Information


What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Job Description


About the team

We are the Canva AI Group, and we’re the team behind the intelligence that powers Canva’s AI experiences. From content generation to smart Q&A and brainstorming, we create the end-to-end journeys that make AI feel intuitive, magical, and useful. 

Our vision is to create the most natural and powerful conversational experience for our users - helping them design, write, ideate, and bring their ideas to life with ease. The work we do is at the core of Canva’s success, and it plays a vital role in our ambition to become the world’s leading visual communication platform.

About the role

We’re looking for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer to help reimagine how people interact with Canva. You’ll work on the AI that powers our conversational experiences, building on top of large models and designing scalable systems that can be used across the product.

This is a deeply hands-on role where you’ll be writing code, shaping features, and working side by side with software engineers. You’ll take ideas from prototype to production, partnering with teams across Canva to deliver ML-driven features that feel seamless and genuinely helpful.

If you’re excited about crafting AI that feels human, useful, and empowering.  If you want to work on something that touches millions of users every day, please apply today.

What you will be doing

Building a truly flexible and scalable conversational AI platform, designed to serve diverse use cases without custom implementations (designing modular architecture, scalable model serving, configurable workflows)
Fine-tuning and evaluating LLM-based models to improve performance, efficiency, and user experience
Contributing to platform engineering across both ML and backend systems—this is a highly hands-on role
Exploring integrations with industry-standard tools and best practices like MCP and A2A
Collaborating with cross-functional teams to define and deliver impactful AI features
Mentoring other Machine Learning Engineers and lifting engineering quality and excellence

Make a difference everyday

Join HammondCare and help improve quality of life for older Australians in need. As a not-for-profit, Christian organisation, we provide expert aged care, home care, dementia care and palliative care. Mission-led, we build passionate teams who support older people through life's most difficult circumstances.

With over 90 years of experience, we're known for our relationship-based approach. Whether it's in our homes, hospitals or the community, we bring skill, empathy and innovation to every moment of care.



It's not just care. It's HammondCare.





About The Role

At HammondCare, data is not just information it is a critical enabler of safe, high‑quality care, trusted decision‑making, and responsible innovation.

The Data Architect plays a pivotal role in shaping how data is designed, governed, and used across HammondCare. This role is accountable for defining and evolving both the current‑state and future‑state enterprise data architecture, ensuring our data is trusted, secure, interoperable, and fit‑for‑purpose across clinical, operational, and corporate domains.

Working closely with clinical leaders, business teams, technology partners, and vendors, the Data Architect establishes clear enterprise data standards, patterns, and models that enable reliable reporting, analytics, AI‑enabled insights, and compliance with regulatory and ethical obligations.

This is a hands‑on, outcomes‑focused role translating strategy into practical architecture decisions that improve data quality, reduce risk, and enable HammondCare to scale responsibly while staying true to our mission of care.



This is a permanent full-time role working at St Leonards with hybrid opportunities.





What we can offer you

We care about your wellbeing at HammondCare and as a result offer a wide range of health and lifestyle benefits including:


NFP salary packaging ($15,900 tax free) plus meals and entertainment benefit (up to $2,650 tax free



Additional leave purchase options and 14 weeks paid parental leave



Flexible working options



Discounts on everyday products and services



Discounted healthcare memberships - access to network of fitness centres and pools for employees and families



Free access to independent counselling support for employees and families



Health & Wellbeing programmes



Reward & Recognition programmes



Referral bonuses



Career and professional development opportunities





Key Outcomes


Establish a clear, enterprise‑wide view of data flows and architecture across clinical and corporate systems, vendors, and integration patterns, reducing duplication and unmanaged complexity

Enable safe and effective use of data by balancing accessibility and usability with strong privacy, security, and regulatory compliance obligations

Improve data consistency, quality, and trust, addressing known issues across source systems and interfaces to support reliable reporting, analytics, and decision‑making

Deliver pragmatic data architecture that supports near‑term organisational priorities, while progressively enabling a sustainable, scalable target‑state architecture

Simplified and rationalised enterprise data architecture across clinical and corporate platforms

Trusted, well‑governed data that is accessible for care, operations, and insight - without compromising privacy or compliance

Improved data quality and consistency across core systems, reducing risk and rework

A clear architectural pathway that meets today's needs while enabling HammondCare's long‑term data and AI strategy



Responsibilities


Enterprise Data Architecture (Current & Target State)

Data Modelling & Domain Design

Integration & Interoperability

Data Platform & Technology Architecture

Governance, Security, Privacy & Compliance by Design

Architecture Assurance & Delivery Enablement Stakeholder Partnership & Communication




About you


At least 7 year's demonstrated experience operating as a Data Architect or senior data engineering/architecture leader in complex, regulated environments (such as health, aged care, government, or large enterprises), with accountability for enterprise‑wide outcomes

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data & Analytics, or a related discipline - or equivalent practical experience delivering at scale

Strong capability in enterprise data modelling, integration architecture, and modern data platform patterns, with the ability to balance architectural rigour and real‑world constraints

Proven experience designing data architectures that enable analytics, reporting, and AI‑enabled use cases, while maintaining trust, safety, and compliance

Working knowledge of data governance and stewardship, including metadata management, lineage, data quality, classification, privacy, and information lifecycle management

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence, guide, and build alignment without formal authority, particularly across clinical, operational, and technology teams

Clear and confident communication, documentation, and decision‑making skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts in plain English for executive and clinical audiences

Architecture and/or data certifications are desirable (e.g. TOGAF, Azure or AWS data certifications, DAMA/CDMP), but practical experience and sound judgement are equally valued



Our Culture

HammondCare is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity and inclusion. We strive to create an equal employment environment for all.

Responsibilities
Support critical data engineering and business intelligence initiatives to drive strategic decisions and product development.
Deliver scalable analytical services to enhance client outcomes and provide key insights for portfolio management.
Join a team where your expertise is indispensable and highly valued.

Data Engineer - Commercial Risk Analytics

An exciting opportunity has become available for a passionate and innovative professional to join our dynamic Commercial Risk Analytics Team. In this role, you will be transforming data into actionable insights that drive business success, while ensuring the detailed integration of cutting-edge technology and strategic analytics.

This is a hybrid role with the flexibility to work both virtually and from our Aon offices.

Aon is in the business of better decisions

At Aon, we shape decisions for the better to protect and enrich the lives of people around the world.

As an organization, we are united through trust as one inclusive, diverse team, and we are passionate about helping our colleagues and clients succeed.

What the day will look like

Data Assets Design: Develop and enhance data assets tailored to business needs, supporting analytics and intelligence projects.
Automated Pipelines: Create and lead automated data ingestion pipelines using Databricks with AWS S3, and SSIS with Microsoft Azure.
Data Quality: Implement routines for data cleaning and standardization, using Python in Databricks notebooks for quality assurance.
SQL Development: Craft SQL scripts for data views, optimizing data flows to introduce new report dimensions and metrics.
Deployment & Monitoring: Deploy notebooks and scripts in production, configure cloud auto-scaling, and supervise performance. Integrate API solutions for GenAI module triggers.
Troubleshooting: Address data ingestion and processing issues, ensuring smooth report generation in PowerBI.
Collaboration: Work closely with data scientists, domain experts, and commercial risk leaders to build data infrastructure to support a broad variety of client risk analytics and business intelligence projects.
Technical Translation: Convert business requirements into technical specifications.

How this opportunity is different

As an Aon colleague, you will never be limited by your job title. Instead, you will learn and grow alongside some of the most versatile individuals in the business, who genuinely want you to be the best version of yourself.

We honestly believe that our culture is different, with authentic and inquisitive individuals who each bring something unique to our team.

Skills and experience that will lead to success

This role seeks for a forward-thinking Data Engineer to support our growing Commercial Risk Analytics team. Additionally, you will have the following:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics, or business intelligence
Proficiency in SQL, Python, and cloud-based ETL tools like SSIS or Alteryx
Hands-on experience with Databricks (or similar platforms)
Knowledge of data architectures (data warehouses, lakehouse, and data mesh)
Skilled in Power BI for visualization and dashboard troubleshooting
Ability to document workflows, conduct testing, and ensure data quality
Experience integrating APIs and GenAI modules is a plus
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, IT, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s a plus)

Preferred

Databricks certification
Exposure to MLOps and Natural Language Processing (NLP)

How we support our colleagues

In addition to our comprehensive benefits package, we encourage an inclusive workforce. Plus, our agile environment allows you to manage your wellbeing and work/life balance, ensuring you can be your best self at Aon. Furthermore, all colleagues enjoy two “Global Wellbeing Days” each year, encouraging you to take time to focus on yourself.  We offer a variety of working style solutions for our colleagues as well. 

 

Our continuous learning culture inspires and equips you to learn, share and grow, helping you achieve your fullest potential. As a result, at Aon, you are more connected, more relevant, and more valued.

You can request accommodations by emailing us at ReasonableAccommodations@Aon.com or your recruiter. We will work with you to meet your needs and ensure a fair and equitable experience.

About the opportunity
We are hiring senior AI Product Delivery Leads to support strong growth across Australia. You will lead small delivery squads building production AI systems for major organisations.
 
Working alongside experienced engineers, you will shape solutions, guide technical direction, and ensure systems move from concept through to live deployment.
 
This is a client-facing consulting role combining product thinking, technical fluency, and disciplined delivery.
 
 
The role
You will lead cross-functional AI squads and act as the delivery owner across complex engagements. You will work closely with engineers, architects, and client stakeholders to translate business problems into production AI systems.

Guide architectural decisions alongside engineering leads
Support engineers building RAG pipelines, agent workflows, and AI applications

Team structure

Solution Engineers
AI Engineers

Experience leading engineering squads delivering production software

About the team

The Quantum Control Solutions provides the technical expertise to support all customer engagements, business development, and sales. Our work focuses on delivering solutions to commercially relevant use cases for industry and government customers and partners. We help translate customer problems into quantum computing solutions through algorithm design and implementation on real devices. The team strives to deliver maximum value by modeling realistic and relevant scenarios in close collaboration with our customers.

About the role


As a Solutions Engineer, you’ll be responsible for developing solutions for customer-driven problems in a variety of quantum technology platforms using Q-CTRL’s product suite.

What you'll be doing:

Take part in all stages of engagement with customers. You will participate in meetings, identify customers’ needs and challenges, deliver solution demonstrations, and provide exceptional support to customers throughout the lifetime of projects and partnerships.
Research and implement innovative and high-quality solutions for applications of quantum computing. You will support the integration of innovative solution features into Q-CTRL’s quantum infrastructure software products.
Write reports on project progress and outcomes in accessible and technically accurate language.
Collaborate closely with your colleagues across a variety of teams including Quantum Research, Sales, Product, Engineering, and with external partners.
Lead critical deliverables in customer or project delivery, and take initiative in technical and strategic decisions.
Ideally you'll have:

A PhD in Physics, Computer Science, or a closely related discipline OR at least 3 years of industry experience or conducting research in a relevant field. 
A keen understanding of near-term quantum algorithms, and experience designing or tailoring algorithm components to support resource efficiency or hardware implementation.
It would be fantastic if you have these skills/experience but not essential:

Experience implementing quantum computing solutions for end user applications in finance, transport, logistics, quantum chemistry, quantum simulations, or drug discovery (this list is not exhaustive and we welcome applicants with experience in other industries). 
Experience implementing algorithms on quantum hardware and familiarity with Qiskit.
Familiarity with classical optimization methods, quantum chemistry, quantum machine learning, or quantum simulations.

About Q-CTRL

Q-CTRL is the global leader in AI-powered quantum control infrastructure software. We build the tools that make quantum technology useful, solving the hardest challenges in quantum computing and quantum sensing to deliver real-world impact.

Founded in 2017, we operate globally with offices in Sydney, Los Angeles, San Francisco, Berlin, and Oxford. Our teams bring together technical and multi-disciplinary expertise across the product lifecycle, and we’re hiring talent to help scale every part of the business. We work quickly to turn cutting-edge science into deployable technology.

In 2024 we raised US$113 million in Series B funding, the largest aggregate investment for a quantum software company. Six months later we delivered the first commercial quantum advantage with Ironstone Opal, our field-validated quantum navigation solution for defense and industry.

At Q-CTRL, we prioritize outcomes over hours. We offer flexibility, equity potential, and competitive benefits that reflect our high-performance culture. If you’re ready to help shape the future of quantum, we’d love to hear from you!

Please be advised that our communications will only come from the @q-ctrl.com domain. All our active job postings are available on our company website.

To recruitment agencies, we do not accept unsolicited branded profiles and are not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Job Title: AI Engineer - Agentic AI & Gen AI
Ad Text:
Melbourne / Sydney | Permanent OR Contract

Tenth Revolution Group is partnering with a specialist AI and data consultancy delivering advanced AI solutions into highly regulated enterprise environments.

Due to continued growth, they are looking to appoint multiple AI Engineers to play a key role in building and deploying next-generation AI solutions, with a particular focus on agentic AI systems and generative AI platforms.

This is a hands-on engineering role within a growing AI team responsible for turning emerging AI capabilities into production-grade systems used in complex, real-world environments.

Open to both permanent or contract, Melbourne or Sydney locations.

The Opportunity

You will design, prototype and deploy advanced AI systems that automate complex workflows and support intelligent decision-making.

Working closely with engineers, data specialists and business stakeholders, you will help translate ideas into scalable AI products while contributing to internal frameworks and reusable AI components.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building practical AI systems rather than purely researching them.

Key Responsibilities

Design and deliver end-to-end AI solutions solving complex business challenges

Build and deploy AI agents and automated workflows using modern frameworks

Develop and scale Generative AI platforms using cloud-native AI services

Implement RAG pipelines, orchestration layers and AI-powered workflow automation

Deploy and operate AI solutions in Azure-based environments

Collaborate with engineers, data specialists and stakeholders to deliver production-ready systems

Monitor, optimise and continuously improve deployed AI applications

Skills & Experience

Strong software engineering background with several years building production systems

Hands-on experience developing AI agents, automation workflows or multi-agent systems

Experience working with agent frameworks such as LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI or similar

Proven experience designing and deploying Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures

Strong experience building AI systems using LLMs, embeddings and vector-based retrieval

Experience deploying AI workloads on Azure, including services within the Azure AI ecosystem (e.g. Azure AI Foundry, Azure AI Search or similar)

Familiarity with cloud-native AI architecture, orchestration frameworks and API integrations

Exposure to knowledge graphs, advanced retrieval techniques or orchestration patterns is advantageous

Microsoft certifications (particularly Azure AI Engineer or related) are highly regarded

Why Apply?

Join a growing AI engineering team building advanced AI capabilities

Work on agent-based AI systems and production GenAI solutions

Strong technical ownership with opportunity to shape AI frameworks and tooling

Collaborative environment with strong focus on learning and development

Apply now or contact Tenth Revolution Group for a confidential discussion.

Contact:

Neros Gorges

Ad Text:
About the ABC

The ABC is the nation's most trusted and independent source of Australian conversations, culture, and stories. With over 4,000 employees from diverse backgrounds across over 50 locations around Australia and overseas, we are proud to create, curate and deliver high-quality content that informs, educates and entertains Australian communities.

About the Role

This is a great opportunity to design, build and maintain trusted, high-quality data assets that help the ABC unlock the full value of its data. The role enables self-service analytics and supports confident, data-informed decision making across the organisation. Working collaboratively with teams across the ABC, you will combine strong data engineering capability with a passion for transparency, governance and data literacy. You will play a key role in strengthening how data is understood, trusted and used to support ABC’s audiences and strategic goals.

Key Accountabilities

Design, build and maintain integrated, scalable and well-documented data assets using SQL, dbt and best practice data modelling techniques to support self-service analytics.
Establish and maintain robust testing, monitoring and validation frameworks to ensure data accuracy, completeness and reliability.
Under broad direction and in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders, contribute to the design, implementation and support of enterprise data and analytics solutions, including data acquisition, transformation patterns, data models, source-to-target mapping and reporting specifications.
Perform data profiling to understand data quality characteristics, document issues and recommend practical approaches for improvement.
Create clear and accessible documentation for data processes and models, ensuring enterprise data assets are discoverable, understandable and maintainable over time.
Promote and apply data governance best practices to support consistency, security and appropriate use of data across the organisation.
Translate business problems into effective technical data solutions using sound engineering judgement and best practice.

About You

Relevant tertiary qualifications or equivalent practical experience with strong expertise in modern cloud data warehouse technologies such as Snowflake, Google BigQuery, Databricks etc.
Demonstrated experience working with cloud-based data platforms and ecosystems, including data warehouses, ETL tools and data visualisation solutions.
Proven ability to design, develop and maintain complex data models and transformations within a data warehouse environment.
Advanced SQL capability, including building complex transformation logic, supported by experience with version control and collaborative development practices.
Hands-on experience delivering data projects end to end using Agile ways of working and familiarity with scrum delivery approaches.
Strong understanding of data governance practices, including data quality, metadata management, data security, privacy and master data management.
Experience implementing data validation, monitoring and quality management approaches to ensure trusted and reliable data.

For further information on this position please contact Jamie Nedved, Digital Talent Acquisition Lead via email nedved.jamie@abc.net.au 

Full Position Description: Analytics Engineer.pdf

We respectfully request that Recruitment Agencies do not submit applications for this position.

In line with our focus on diversity, applications are strongly encouraged from Indigenous Australians, people from a range of cultural and linguistic backgrounds, people with disability and LGBTIQA+ individuals. The ABC also aims to achieve a gender-balanced workforce

To request recruitment and selection information in alternate formats, request adjustments, or if you have accessibility requirements, please contact accessibility@abc.net.au

For more information on inclusive employee networks within the ABC please refer to Life Page on ABC LinkedIn. 

For more information on working at the ABC visit abc.net.au/careers

About the ABC

The ABC is the nation's most trusted and independent source of Australian conversations, culture, and stories. With over 4,000 employees from diverse backgrounds across over 50 locations around Australia and overseas, we are proud to create, curate and deliver high-quality content that informs, educates and entertains Australian communities.

About the Role

This is a great opportunity to design, build and maintain trusted, high-quality data assets that help the ABC unlock the full value of its data. The role enables self-service analytics and supports confident, data-informed decision making across the organisation. Working collaboratively with teams across the ABC, you will combine strong data engineering capability with a passion for transparency, governance and data literacy. You will play a key role in strengthening how data is understood, trusted and used to support ABC’s audiences and strategic goals.

Key Accountabilities

Design, build and maintain integrated, scalable and well-documented data assets using SQL, dbt and best practice data modelling techniques to support self-service analytics.
Establish and maintain robust testing, monitoring and validation frameworks to ensure data accuracy, completeness and reliability.
Under broad direction and in collaboration with internal and external stakeholders, contribute to the design, implementation and support of enterprise data and analytics solutions, including data acquisition, transformation patterns, data models, source-to-target mapping and reporting specifications.
Perform data profiling to understand data quality characteristics, document issues and recommend practical approaches for improvement.
Create clear and accessible documentation for data processes and models, ensuring enterprise data assets are discoverable, understandable and maintainable over time.
Promote and apply data governance best practices to support consistency, security and appropriate use of data across the organisation.
Translate business problems into effective technical data solutions using sound engineering judgement and best practice.

About You

Relevant tertiary qualifications or equivalent practical experience with strong expertise in modern cloud data warehouse technologies such as Snowflake, Google BigQuery, Databricks etc.
Demonstrated experience working with cloud-based data platforms and ecosystems, including data warehouses, ETL tools and data visualisation solutions.
Proven ability to design, develop and maintain complex data models and transformations within a data warehouse environment.
Advanced SQL capability, including building complex transformation logic, supported by experience with version control and collaborative development practices.
Hands-on experience delivering data projects end to end using Agile ways of working and familiarity with scrum delivery approaches.
Strong understanding of data governance practices, including data quality, metadata management, data security, privacy and master data management.
Experience implementing data validation, monitoring and quality management approaches to ensure trusted and reliable data.

About the Role


This is not a traditional software engineering position. TechXcel Applied AI Studio is hiring Agentic Engineers to join our Applied AI Division as we transition to an agentic-first engineering model, a fundamentally different approach to how software is designed, built, and delivered.

As an Agentic Engineer, you won’t spend your days writing code line by line. Instead, you’ll plan, architect, and orchestrate autonomous AI agents that write and construct code on your behalf. Your role is to direct, evaluate, and redirect those agents in real time, shaping the output, not manually producing it.

Critically, this role places direct client engagement at the centre of your engineering work. You won’t be shielded from clients by layers of project managers or business analysts. You’ll work alongside clients continuously, translating emerging agentic capabilities into real-time delivery outcomes.


What You’ll Do

Design, deploy, and manage autonomous multi-agent systems and orchestration pipelines for client projects

Direct and evaluate AI agent outputs in real time, making rapid decisions about quality, direction, and client alignment

Develop prompt architecture and context engineering strategies at production scale

Build reliability and evaluation frameworks to ensure consistent, high-quality agentic outputs

Integrate agentic LLM tooling into commercial delivery workflows across diverse client environments

Engage directly and continuously with clients as a core part of your technical role, understanding their evolving needs, communicating what’s achievable, and adjusting delivery in the moment

Operate in dynamic, non-linear delivery cycles driven by agent outputs rather than traditional sprint-based pipelines





About You

Essential

Demonstrated experience or strong aptitude in orchestrating AI agents and designing multi-agent workflows

Ability to evaluate and redirect AI agent outputs in real time against defined objectives

Proficiency in prompt engineering and context architecture at scale

Confident and capable in direct client-facing engagement, this is a primary function of the role, not a secondary duty

Strong commercial acumen with the ability to connect engineering delivery to tangible client outcomes

Adaptability and hunger to work within a rapidly evolving AI tooling ecosystem


Desirable

Experience with LLM-based agent frameworks (e.g. OpenClaw, Paperclip or equivalent)

Background in AI/ML engineering, software engineering, or a related technical discipline

Experience working in consulting, professional services, or client-delivery environments

Familiarity with reliability and evaluation frameworks for generative AI systems





Why TechXcel Applied AI Studio


We’re not layering AI on top of old ways of working. We’re restructuring our entire engineering model around an agentic-first approach because we believe this is where the industry is heading, and we’re moving now, not later.

Be at the forefront of the agentic engineering paradigm, not a side project, but our core operating model

Work directly with clients on real delivery challenges, not isolated behind product backlogs

Join a team that’s investing in this transition with full organisational commitment from the board down

Competitive salary, benefits, and the opportunity to shape a new discipline from the ground up





How to Apply


Click Apply Now and submit your CV along with a brief cover letter addressing two things:

Your experience or perspective on agentic AI engineering, how have you worked with or thought about AI agents in a delivery context?

A specific example of how you’ve engaged directly with clients or stakeholders to shape a technical outcome

We’re reviewing applications on a rolling basis. Don’t wait.

Are you a Computer Scientist or Software Engineer with a passion for Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning and the engineering discipline to turn research-driven ideas into efficient, robust, and usable software?

We are seeking a mid-level software engineering and AI professional to join our team. This position provides the opportunity to work with a high performing team on projects delivering AI-enabled software that contributes directly to Australia’s sovereign Defence capability and national security.

About us

Swordfish operates at the intersection of advanced technology and Australian Defence capability. We are a Research Engineering Services company that delivers transformative innovation to Government, the Australian Defence Force, and Defence industry clients, helping solve complex technology challenges in support of Australia’s security and national interests.

Our Defence expertise includes Electronic Warfare, Combat and Mission Systems, Autonomous Systems, Operational Analysis, Cyber Security, Aerospace and various applications of Modeling and Simulation. Swordfish currently employs a team of over 85 talented and passionate professionals with skills spanning many disciplines but all sharing common foundational Agile Software Engineering skills, a collaborative work ethos and commitment to continual learning.

We offer the opportunity for the right candidate to work amongst high-calibre, passionate and collaborative teammates, employing Agile software engineering practices, a variety of modern tool stacks, and cutting-edge AI/ML technologies to create solutions that have a real impact to the ADF.

At Swordfish we place the highest value on our people. We see opportunity in what each individual brings to the table. We act with integrity and support each other to achieve our best. We strive to maintain an open, honest working environment by fostering a culture of collaboration and trust that welcomes new ideas and alternative solutions. We are passionate about what we do and eagerly welcome newcomers to our teams.



What we are looking for

We are seeking a motivated and experienced Software Engineer with strong AI/ML interest and capability, and a passion for delivering innovative, high-quality engineered solutions.

You are someone who thrives in technically demanding environments at the intersection of AI, applied sciences, and software engineering. Your experience in areas such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative AI, and agentic AI, combined with strong software engineering skills and Agile development practices, will be instrumental in translating emerging ideas into practical, reliable Defence capability.

Experience in software development, including coding, testing, automation and deployment is essential. The ideal candidate will have proficiency in a broad range of AI techniques and technologies, programming languages, modern frameworks, software architectures, Agile engineering practices and DevOps.

Responsibilities will include stakeholder engagement, leading AI/ML software design and implementation, supporting the professional development of junior staff and supporting company leadership achieve strategic goals.

You will join our multi-disciplinary teams working closely with Defence researchers to solve complex, real-world problems in areas including aerospace guidance and control, combat management systems, multi-disciplinary optimisation, human identity verification, and resilient distributed information systems.

What you will do

Work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team, including close engagement with product owners and program leads

Contribute directly to the prototyping, implementation, testing, and delivery of AI-enabled applications

Engage with clients and project teams to analyse requirements, identify opportunities, and improve engineering practices and processes

Help drive project success through effective planning, delivery, communication, and responsiveness to client and partner feedback

Maintain awareness of emerging developments in AI, ML, and software engineering, and assess their relevance to current and future Defence projects

Support the development of junior engineers through mentoring, technical guidance, and code reviews



 Essential skills and experience

3+ years of professional experience in Software Engineering

Experience in AI research and development, including computer vision, large language models, natural language processing, machine learning, and data science

Software Engineering including, architecture, design, implementation, testing, and integration

Proficiency in Python programming, and familiarity with packages like Numpy, Pandas, scikit-learn, Tensorflow, and PyTorch

Configuration management, tools and practices

Excellent oral and written communication skills, with an ability to foster effective stakeholder relationships

Adaptability and resilience in a dynamically changing environment

Commitment towards ongoing self-improvement and professional development

 Desirable skills

Experience in managing complex Software Engineering and AI projects

Leadership, including managing and mentoring junior team members and fostering effective collaboration between cross-functional and multi-agency teams.

Object-Oriented software development in C++, C#, Java and/or Python

Docker container development and deployment

Microservice Architectures

Knowledge of cloud computing platforms and experience with big data technologies is a plus.

Atlassian tools; Confluence, Jira, and BitBucket

Qualifications

Bachelor’s or higher degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, or related fields. (Higher AI/ML, and mathematics qualifications and/or experience would be highly advantageous)

Australian Citizenship. Successful candidates must be eligible to hold and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance. Please see the following URL for eligibility requirements: Australian Government Security Vetting Agency  

Location

Swordfish is headquartered in the Adelaide CBD with our teams operate from Adelaide and Edinburgh. If you have specific requirements, please discuss them during your interview.

Benefits

Interesting and rewarding work

Fun, and supportive culture

37.5 hour working week with flexible work arrangements and work-life balance

Support for your professional development

Attractive remuneration

Relocation support if you live interstate

LaserBond continues to grow nationwide, with facilities in NSW, QLD, SA, VIC and WA

Automation Engineer

LaserBond | Sydney NSW | Full-time

Technical documentation and procedures
Key Responsibilities

Collaborate with production teams, R&D and project engineers
Liaise with external vendors, contractors and suppliers

Assist with continuous improvement initiatives for automation systems

Degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechatronics, Automation Engineering or similar

Experience with PLC programming (Allen-Bradley preferred)

Experience with commissioning, FAT/SAT and industrial equipment

Ability to work collaboratively with engineering and production teams

Professional with strong communication skills

Join an innovative Australian manufacturing company

Career growth within a high-tech engineering organisation

94.7% of AI projects don’t make it to production.

I have zero data to back this up.

Do you know what the actual figure is?

The manager I’m recruiting for will have a more informed view on what the actual percentage may be.

If you’re interested in having an intellectual argument (or many) about such things you should keep reading.

Machine Learning Engineer wanted! (not exclusively for arguing!).

Some real AI work is being done.

Yes, that’s right – live ML models in production, customers paying, challenges with scale, evolving datasets, model valuation, and all the practical realities that come with it.

You’re probably working in machine learning or deep learning somewhere highly technical now.

Maybe you love what you’re doing but feel constrained by tooling, data access, or process.

Maybe you enjoy modelling but don’t want to drift into pure MLOps.

Maybe you just want to work with strong technical people on meaningful problems (everyone is hands on in this team).

Over the next 6 months and beyond, you’ll be training and iterating on ML / DL models using real datasets, working on model valuation and experimentation, contributing to 3D modelling and computer vision problems (3D experience is a big bonus, not a requirement), helping improve how data is queried and sampled as the team moves away from manual workflows toward more dynamic, database-driven access, and strengthening parts of the ML lifecycle from training through to evaluation, iteration and deployment.

This isn’t a notebook-only role, and it’s not a production-only MLOps role either. It’s about building models and improving the systems around them.

You don’t need encyclopaedic knowledge of recent vision research, but as someone in ML you should have a reasonable understanding of things like CNNs vs Transformers and be curious about modern approaches.

You’ll have personally trained ML or DL models on meaningful datasets, iterated on performance through experimentation, worked on evaluation or model valuation, and taken models beyond toy projects or university capstones.

On the engineering side, you’ll be comfortable working in shared Python codebases using Git, building repeatable training and evaluation workflows, improving existing systems rather than starting from scratch each time, and contributing to ML infrastructure without being a full-time platform engineer. SQL experience is important – the team is moving toward more dynamic, query-driven data workflows.

Nice to haves include 3D computer vision or 3D modelling, Airflow, Metaflow or similar orchestration tools, GPU or distributed training, and cloud ML workflows – but these are bonuses, and the team can help you grow here.

You’ll join a highly technical ML team tackling complex geospatial problems at scale. This is an environment for people who enjoy strong technical discussions, pragmatic problem solving, sharing knowledge, and owning problems end to end. Previous hires from this group have gone on to technical leadership roles in startups – growth is very much part of the picture.

An Honours degree or higher in a quantitative or technical discipline (Computer Science, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, Statistics, or similar) is a minimum requirement. Masters, PhD, or equivalent international qualifications are also accepted.
Interview process:

Technical discussion with the hiring manager to understand your approach to ML problems, modelling, data and systems.

Coding Assignment: 1 week to complete take home task (you can use Co-Pilot, just please disclose that you are, for what and why).

Panel interview – Business introductions and deep technical-solutioning (think design and ML engineering).

This role is open to remote, but Eastern seaboard Australia-based candidates only.

Apply or email ronny@theonset.com.au to learn more.

Job Title: Principal Architect – AI Experiences and Enablement
Ad Text:
Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The Opportunity

We’re hiring a Principal Architect to lead how SEEK designs and builds AI‑enabled and agentic experiences at scale. Reporting to the Head of Architecture – Data & AI, you’ll own the vision, reference architectures and guardrails for AI and agentic solutions across SEEK.

You’ll establish the foundations, patterns and standards that enable teams to build agents that are trusted, safe and high‑impact – integrating securely with SEEK systems and delivering measurable value for customers and SEEKers. You’ll work closely with design, data science, engineering, product and architecture teams to turn ideas into scalable, human‑centred AI experiences.

We care more about what you’ve done than your title. You might be a Principal/Staff Architect, AI/ML Architect, Platform or Solution Architect. If you’ve been designing and delivering complex AI or agentic systems, and effectively operating at a senior leadership level, we’d love to talk – even if you haven’t held the “Enterprise Architect” title.

The Team

Sitting within the Data and AI Architecture domain, this role operates centrally across Customer, Enterprise and Platform architecture to drive cohesive strategies and governance. The team provides specialist, cross‑functional leadership to ensure scalable, consistent outcomes across SEEK’s ecosystem. You’ll manage and mentor solution architects, align architecture, uplift capability and strengthen stakeholder engagement.

What you’ll do

Define enterprise‑wide standards, reference architectures and guardrails for AI and agentic experiences.
Shape the end‑to‑end lifecycle of agents – creation, orchestration, monitoring, observability and oversight.
Act as a strategic advisor on AI and agentic adoption, investment and governance.
Enable teams with reusable capabilities, patterns and frameworks to safely accelerate delivery.
Lead and guide solution design for high‑impact, AI‑centric initiatives.
Drive experimentation with emerging frameworks/protocols (e.g. MCP, A2A, AP2) and translate learnings into platform and architecture standards.
Embed trust, security, privacy and responsible AI in all agentic architectures.

Why this role and team?

You’ll shape the next generation of AI‑powered and agentic experiences across APAC, have direct influence on technology and investment decisions, and help establish the foundations used by product, business and engineering teams. You’ll join a high‑performing, inclusive, people‑first culture and have the opportunity to build your profile across the broader APAC AI ecosystem.

If you’ve been designing and delivering real AI and agentic systems at scale, we’d love to hear from you.

Qualifications

About you

Deep experience designing systems with human‑in‑the‑loop interactions, autonomous agents or workflow automation.
Strong knowledge of LLMs, AI/ML systems and agent frameworks (e.g. LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI or similar).
Experience architecting AI platform capabilities (e.g. LLM or MCP gateways/servers) on AWS and/or GCP.
Comfortable with event‑driven, modular architectures, vector databases and modern AI frameworks.
Practical understanding of AI observability, monitoring and evaluation.
Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders, shape strategy and guide organisations through technology change.
Strong communicator and thought leader, with a growth mindset and appetite for experimentation.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The Team 

SEEK’s purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. 

Our Platforms area builds the shared services, infrastructure and developer enablement that underpin SEEK’s experiences for candidates and hirers across APAC. We evolve self-service platform capabilities so teams can ship and operate services reliably, supported by strong observability capabilities.  

You’ll work across an APAC-wide engineering organisation and partner closely with engineering, product, architecture, security, and AI & data leaders. 

The Role 

As a Principal Staff Engineer (Engineering Platforms), you are a technical authority for a major platform area and trusted advisor.  

You’ll provide technical leadership, unblock complex delivery and operational challenges, lift engineering standards across multiple squads, and help ensure platform services remain fit-for-purpose, secure, resilient and aligned to SEEK’s broader technical strategy. 

Key Responsibilities 

Lead technical direction discussions for a major platform area with key stakeholders, aligning on target architecture, priorities and trade-offs. 

Unblock hard problems: deep dive on complex technical issues (delivery, reliability, scaling), and guide teams to sound solutions.
